goal: Scaffold the NestJS backend with all modules, TypeORM dual-database config, entities, and the health endpoint. No frontend code. No seed data (that's Epic 2).

included: TypeORM dual-database config (SQLite local / Postgres prod)
All 5 entity definitions with correct constraints
6 NestJS modules (agencies, spending, geography, disaster, sync, health)
CORS, global validation pipe, health endpoint

assumptions: Node 22+ and pnpm 10+ are available on the development machine
NestJS CLI is available via npx @nestjs/cli
Git repo is already initialized with .gitignore in place
shared/interfaces/index.ts already contains all entity interfaces and ApiResponse<T>
docs/ directory already contains all epic plan files

risks.description: TypeORM better-sqlite3 native module may fail to install on some systems. Mitigation: verify install succeeds in Story 1.
tsconfig paths alias (@shared/*) may not resolve correctly at runtime without additional NestJS config (nest-cli.json compilerOptions). Build agent must test this.
Epic doc lists sync.controller.ts in file tree but CLI command says --no-controller. This is a contradiction — resolved by NOT creating sync.controller.ts in Epic 1 (sync endpoints belong to Epic 2).

contradictions.description: Step 8 says 'npx nest g module sync --no-controller' but the file tree shows 'sync/sync.controller.ts'. The correct approach for Epic 1 is to NOT create sync.controller.ts — sync endpoints are defined in Epic 2. The sync module only needs the module and service scaffold.
contradictions.resolution: Skip sync.controller.ts in Epic 1. Only create sync.module.ts and sync.service.ts as empty scaffolds.

contradictions.description: File tree lists 'common/api-response.interface.ts' but ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts. Duplicating it would create drift risk.
contradictions.resolution: Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces in the interceptor rather than duplicating the interface in common/.

stories.title: Scaffold NestJS project and install dependencies
stories.goal: Create the backend/ NestJS application with all required packages installed
outputs: NestJS project at backend/ that compiles and starts with default scaffold
All required dependencies installed: @nestjs/typeorm, typeorm, better-sqlite3, pg, @nestjs/config, class-validator, class-transformer, @nestjs/schedule
Dev dependency: @types/better-sqlite3
.env.example with DATABASE_URL, FRONTEND_URL, NODE_ENV
acceptanceCriteria: `cd backend && pnpm install` completes without errors
`pnpm start:dev` starts the default NestJS scaffold (HelloWorld)
All 9 runtime dependencies appear in package.json
.env.example exists with the 3 required variables
better-sqlite3 native module compiles successfully
stories.notes: The repo root already has .gitignore, README.md, docs/, and shared/. Skip those. Only create backend/.

stories.title: Configure tsconfig path alias and nest-cli.json for @shared
stories.goal: Wire up the @shared/* path alias so backend code can import from shared/interfaces
outputs: tsconfig.json paths entry for @shared/* → ../shared/*
nest-cli.json compilerOptions.plugins or compilerOptions.assets configured if needed
Build compiles and resolves @shared/interfaces imports
acceptanceCriteria: tsconfig.json contains paths: { '@shared/*': ['../shared/*'] }
A test import `import { Agency } from '@shared/interfaces'` compiles without error
`pnpm build` succeeds with the path alias configured
stories.notes: NestJS may need nest-cli.json compilerOptions to handle out-of-tree paths at build time. The builder may not follow tsconfig paths. May need to add a webpack plugin or use tsconfig-paths. Build agent should verify build + runtime resolution.

stories.title: Configure TypeORM dual-database (SQLite / Postgres)
stories.goal: Set up TypeORM with environment-based switching between SQLite (dev) and Postgres (prod)
outputs: typeorm.config.ts with getTypeOrmConfig function
AppModule registers TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync using ConfigService
SQLite mode: better-sqlite3, database: data/fedspend.sqlite, synchronize: true
Postgres mode: triggered by DATABASE_URL env var, ssl, synchronize: false, migrationsRun: true
data/ directory creation handled (gitignored)
acceptanceCriteria: With no DATABASE_URL, app starts and creates data/fedspend.sqlite
TypeORM config function switches to postgres config when DATABASE_URL is set
AppModule uses ConfigModule.forRoot() with isGlobal: true
Entity glob pattern resolves correctly: __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}'
No entity tables exist yet (entities come in S05) but TypeORM connects without error
stories.notes: Risk R1: better-sqlite3 native compilation. The entity glob pattern needs to be tested — NestJS CLI scaffold puts app.module.ts in src/, so __dirname at runtime is dist/, meaning the glob should be __dirname + '/**/*.entity{.ts,.js}'. The epic doc uses '/../**/*.entity' which may be wrong. Build agent should verify.

stories.title: Create 6 NestJS modules with controller/service scaffolds
stories.goal: Generate all 6 modules (agencies, spending, geography, disaster, sync, health) with their controllers and services
outputs: 6 modules registered in AppModule
agencies, spending, geography, disaster modules each have controller + service
sync module has module + service only (NO controller — contradicts file tree, follows CLI command)
health module has module + controller only (no service needed)
Empty dto/ directories created for agencies, spending, geography, disaster
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 modules appear in AppModule imports
`pnpm start:dev` starts without errors with all modules registered
sync module does NOT have a controller file (contradiction resolution)
Each module that has entities (S05) imports TypeOrmModule.forFeature — placeholder ready
stories.notes: CONTRADICTION RESOLVED: File tree shows sync/sync.controller.ts but Step 8 CLI command says --no-controller. Following the CLI command — no sync controller in Epic 1. Sync endpoints are Epic 2's concern.

stories.title: Define all 5 TypeORM entities with constraints
stories.goal: Create all entity classes with correct columns, types, relations, and unique constraints
outputs: 5 entity files with @Entity decorators
All columns match data-model.md exactly
All monetary values use integer type (cents)
Unique constraints: SpendingRecord(agencyId, fiscalYear, quarter, awardTypeLabel), GeoSpendingSnapshot(stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope), DisasterFundingRecord(defGroup, stateCode)
FK: SpendingRecord.agencyId → Agency, GeoSpendingSnapshot.agencyId → Agency (nullable)
Each module registers entities via TypeOrmModule.forFeature([...])
acceptanceCriteria: `pnpm start:dev` creates data/fedspend.sqlite with all 5 tables
Table columns match data-model.md field definitions
Unique constraints exist on the 3 composite keys
SpendingRecord.agencyId has FK to Agency.id
GeoSpendingSnapshot.agencyId has nullable FK to Agency.id
All monetary columns (obligatedAmount, outlayAmount, femaObligated, fedSpendingObligated, perCapita) are integer type
recoveryRatio is numeric/float type (not integer)
stories.notes: Careful with GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint — agencyId can be null. TypeORM needs @Unique decorator with the array syntax, and SQLite treats NULLs as unique in constraints by default, which is actually what we want (null = all-agencies combined per state/fy/scope). The build agent should verify this behavior.

stories.title: Response wrapper interceptor and @Paginated() decorator
stories.goal: Create the common response wrapper infrastructure that will be used by all paginated list endpoints
outputs: ResponseWrapperInterceptor that wraps paginated responses as { data, meta }
Single-item endpoints return T directly (no wrapping)
@Paginated() decorator to mark endpoints that should be wrapped
Interceptor registered globally in AppModule
acceptanceCriteria: @Paginated() decorator can be applied to controller methods
ResponseWrapperInterceptor checks for @Paginated() metadata
Paginated endpoints return { data: T[], meta: { total, page, pageSize } }
Non-paginated endpoints return T directly unchanged
Interceptor is registered as a global provider in AppModule
Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces, not duplicated locally
stories.notes: CONTRADICTION RESOLVED: Epic file tree shows common/api-response.interface.ts but ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts. Do NOT duplicate — import from @shared/interfaces. The interceptor needs a way to get total count from the service layer. Common patterns: (1) service returns { data, total }, (2) interceptor wraps after the fact. Build agent should choose the cleaner approach.

stories.title: CORS, validation pipe, and health endpoint
stories.goal: Wire up CORS, global validation, and the GET /health endpoint that verifies database connectivity
outputs: main.ts: CORS enabled via FRONTEND_URL env var (fallback '*' in dev)
main.ts: Global ValidationPipe with whitelist: true, transform: true
GET /health returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO, database: 'connected' }
Health endpoint checks TypeORM DataSource connection is active
acceptanceCriteria: CORS headers present in responses (Origin matches FRONTEND_URL)
Without FRONTEND_URL set, CORS allows '*'
Invalid request body with extra properties has those properties stripped (whitelist)
`GET /health` returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: '<ISO>', database: 'connected' }
`GET /health` returns { database: 'disconnected' } and appropriate status when DB is unreachable
stories.notes: Health check needs to inject DataSource from @nestjs/typeorm and call dataSource.query('SELECT 1') or check .isInitialized. Decide whether to use @nestjs/terminus or keep it simple — for a portfolio project, the simple approach in the epic doc is fine.

stories.title: End-to-end verification of Epic 1
stories.goal: Start the full backend and verify all acceptance criteria pass

files: All backend/ files (verification only, no new files)

outputs: Verified working backend with all acceptance criteria met
acceptanceCriteria: `pnpm start:dev` starts without errors
`GET /health` returns status JSON with database: 'connected'
data/fedspend.sqlite exists with all 5 entity tables
All 6 modules are registered and importable
TypeORM config switches between SQLite and Postgres based on DATABASE_URL
Response wrapper interceptor exists with @Paginated() decorator
CORS enabled via FRONTEND_URL
No frontend code exists in backend/
shared/interfaces/index.ts is importable from backend via @shared/* alias
@Paginated() decorator and interceptor are registered globally
stories.notes: This is a verification-only story. If any criteria fail, the specific issue should be fixed as part of the responsible story, not here. This story confirms the entire epic is complete.

parallelizableGroups.note: Must complete first — all other stories depend on the scaffolded project
All depend only on S01. Can run in parallel. S03 and S04 both modify app.module.ts — may need merge if parallel builds.
Depend on S03 + S04. Can run in parallel.
